titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas G. Buchanan to Lord Wimborne, 19 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas G. Buchanan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: 3 Elizabeth Place, Southern Road, Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
3, Elizabeth Place,
Southern Road,
Cork. May 19.1916
To His Excellency
The Lord Lieutenant
of Ireland, Your Excellency I respectfully beg
to apply for permission
to exhume the remains
of my wife and infant
child, which were interred
in the Douglas Protestant
Cemetery, situated in the
Rural District of Cork
and transfer them to a
new grave about 30 yards
distant in the same
Burial ground in which
the other members of my
family have been interred. The remains of my
wife were interred nearly
12 years ago, and the infant
Girl about 5 years previously. The Cemetery
authorities are quite
agreeable to this
proposal and the Rural District
Council. the Burial Board
for the district will, if
necessary, give their
consent in writing. I am,
Your Excellency's
Obedient Servant . Thomas G. Buchanan
